Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's learning topics spanning measures of central tendency, spread and variability, skewness, and probability distributions, used by instructors to organize content and by learners to navigate what will be covered.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A topic overview page covering descriptive statistics concepts—including measures of central tendency (mean and median), and probability distributions—that students use to preview what they will learn and instructors use to align module objectives with Python-based computation and interpretation tasks.

Artifacts.Name: Descriptive_Statistics_and_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing descriptive statistics and probability distributions—covering mean, median, spread, skewness, and related Python implementation—used by instructors to deliver lectures and by learners to review core concepts and code examples.

Artifacts.Name: Measures of Central Tendency
Artifacts.Description: A reading that introduces mean, median, and mode, explaining how each measure summarises the centre of a dataset so learners can choose the appropriate statistic when beginning an exploratory data analysis.

Artifacts.Name: Measures of Spread and Variability
Artifacts.Description: A reading that covers range, variance, standard deviation, and interquartile range, helping learners understand how to quantify how spread out or dispersed values in a dataset are beyond just its centre.

Artifacts.Name: Skewness and Distribution Shape
Artifacts.Description: A reading that explains distribution shape and skewness — including positive, negative, and symmetric skew — enabling learners to recognise how asymmetry affects the interpretation of summary statistics.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to Probability Distributions
Artifacts.Description: A reading that introduces the concept of probability distributions, distinguishing between discrete and continuous types and explaining how they model uncertainty and the likelihood of outcomes, serving as a conceptual foundation before more specific distributions are studied.

Artifacts.Name: The Normal Distribution
Artifacts.Description: A reading that covers the properties, shape, and real-world applications of the normal distribution, including its bell-curve symmetry and the role of mean and standard deviation, so learners can apply it to naturally occurring data.

Artifacts.Name: The Binomial Distribution
Artifacts.Description: A reading that explains the binomial distribution — its assumptions, formula, and use cases such as binary-outcome experiments — enabling learners to calculate the probability of a given number of successes across a fixed number of trials.

Artifacts.Name: The Poisson Distribution
Artifacts.Description: A reading that describes the Poisson distribution, including its assumptions and applications for modelling the count of events occurring within a fixed interval of time or space, so learners can apply it to real-world rate-based scenarios.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question quiz bank assessing learners' ability to calculate and interpret descriptive statistics (mean, median, outlier sensitivity, distribution shape) and apply relevant NumPy functions in Python, used by instructors to build graded assessments or practice quizzes.
